search_queries_used: site:entnet.org acute otitis externa guideline outcomes reassessment modifying factors 2014
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic drops, antibiotic, steroid, antifungal, wick, culture, imaging, admission, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Persistent symptoms require direct examination and assessment of diabetes, immunocompromise, membrane status, adherence, and alternate diagnosis.
No medicine, dose, investigation, procedure, treatment, referral, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with clinician-established acute otitis externa follow-up; diabetes, immunocompromise, prior radiotherapy, severe pain, cranial neuropathy, and non-intact tympanic membrane require additional pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary, urgent, emergency, or ENT follow-up.
UAE_applicability: International guidance requires UAE antimicrobial, prescribing, microbiology, imaging, ENT, emergency, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official AAO-HNSF 2014 exact symptom and key-action sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2014 update remains the current AAO-HNSF guideline identified; material recency uncertainty remains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact acute-otitis-externa section supports interval pain, itch, discharge, hearing, functional impact, associated features, relevant negatives, modifying factors, vital signs, general appearance, and ENT examination without asserting persistence or complication.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact key-action section supports clinician concern, reviewed specialist information, entered plan, safety net, and arranged follow-up without generating eardrops, antibiotics, wick placement, culture, procedure, or referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

unresolved_source_gaps: Laterality, symptom course, discharge, hearing, canal and membrane findings, diabetes, immune status, medicine, adherence, adverse effects, culture, diagnosis, treatment, complication, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
